label: Mogadishu
sub: Federal capital; Villa Somalia presidential complex; Green Zone perimeter under SNA/ATMIS control

label: Kismayo
sub: Jubbaland capital; strategic port; captured from Al-Shabaab Oct 2012; KDF ATMIS base

label: Baidoa
sub: South West State capital; SNA military HQ for southwest; scene of frequent Al-Shabaab attacks

label: Bosaso
sub: Puntland's commercial hub; key port for Red Sea trade; PMPF paramilitary base

label: Hargeisa
sub: Somaliland capital; self-declared independent state since 1991; relatively stable

label: Garowe
sub: Puntland State capital; administration center; relatively secure from Al-Shabaab

label: Beledweyne
sub: Hiiraan Region capital; front line of 2022 SNA/Macawisley offensive; Ethiopian ATMIS base

label: Dhusamareb
sub: Galmudug State capital; SNA operations hub for central Somalia campaigns

label: Former Bakara Market Base
sub: Al-Shabaab's main Mogadishu revenue hub; controlled 2007–2011; captured by AMISOM Aug 2011

label: Jilib
sub: Al-Shabaab stronghold in Middle Jubba; group's administrative center for Jubba Valley operations

label: Barawe
sub: Coastal town; Al-Shabaab stronghold; historically used for arms smuggling and revenue collection

label: El Bur
sub: Galguduud region; key Al-Shabaab base for central Somalia operations; contested since 2022 SNA offensive

label: Afgoye Corridor
sub: Former IDP corridor west of Mogadishu; historically largest IDP concentration in world; contested Al-Shabaab territory

label: Bu'aale
sub: Middle Jubba capital; seized by Al-Shabaab; key node in Lower/Middle Jubba insurgency area

label: AMISOM/ATMIS Main Base (Mogadishu)
sub: Headquarters of AMISOM/ATMIS Mogadishu sector; Ugandan contingent; transferred to SNA 2024

label: ATMIS Sector 5 Base (Beledweyne)
sub: Ethiopian ATMIS contingent; Hiiraan Region; forward base for operations against Al-Shabaab in central Somalia

label: ATMIS Sector 2 Base (Kismayo)
sub: Kenyan ATMIS contingent; Jubbaland; critical for southern operations and Jubbaland security

label: Mogadishu IDP Settlements
sub: Over 600,000 IDPs in Mogadishu area; dozens of informal settlements; persistent displacement from drought and conflict

label: Lower Shabelle Famine Zone
sub: Site of 2011 UN famine declaration; IPC Phase 4/5 food insecurity; chronic displacement and Al-Shabaab access restrictions

label: Bay-Bakool Drought Zone
sub: Bay and Bakool regions: epicenter of 2011 famine and 2022 near-famine; chronic drought vulnerability; humanitarian access constrained by Al-Shabaab

label: Gulf of Aden Piracy Zone
sub: Primary piracy area 2008–2012; resurgence 2023–24; high traffic chokepoint for global shipping

label: Indian Ocean Piracy Zone
sub: Deep water piracy area; MV Ruen hijacked Nov 2023; mother-ship tactic used for Indian Ocean raids

label: Eyl (Historic Pirate Base)
sub: Puntland coastal town; main base for piracy operations during 2008–2012 peak era; ransoms negotiated here

label: Haradhere (Pirate Anchorage)
sub: Central coast; second major pirate anchorage; vessels held here during ransom negotiations

label: Zoobe Junction (Oct 2017 Bombing)
sub: Site of deadliest attack in Somali history; truck bomb killed 587+ people on Oct 14, 2017

label: El Adde (KDF Base, Jan 2016)
sub: Site of deadliest Al-Shabaab attack on AMISOM; 140-180 Kenyan soldiers killed January 2016

label: Merca
sub: Lower Shabelle coastal town; Al-Shabaab controls surrounding rural areas; SNA presence in town center

label: Jowhar
sub: Hirshabelle State capital; Middle Shabelle; government-controlled town; SNA forward base for Middle Shabelle operations

label: Bulo Burto
sub: Hirshabelle; rural area north of Jowhar; Al-Shabaab tax collection and ambush corridor

label: Hobyo (Piracy Coastal Base)
sub: Central Somali coast; historically used for pirate operations; small port enabling mother-ship refueling

label: Berbera (Somaliland Port)
sub: Strategic Somaliland port; DP World (UAE) operates container terminal; Ethiopia MOU (Jan 2024) targeted Berbera for sea access; Israel granted access rights post-Dec 2025 recognition

label: Oswein SNA Base Overrun (Aug 2023)
sub: Al-Shabaab overran three SNA forward bases at Oswein, north of Mogadishu in August 2023, killing an estimated 150-178 SNA fighters; government denied losses

label: War-Iise (Gorgor Op, Mar 2026)
sub: Site of Gorgor Commando targeted operation (March 24, 2026) neutralizing al-Shabaab cells in Middle Shabelle; part of Operation Rolling Thunder / Operation Bader Ramadan offensive

label: Bulunagaad (SNA/AUSSOM Op, Mar 2026)
sub: Joint SNA and AUSSOM/UPDF ground-and-air operation killed 30+ al-Shabaab fighters at Bulunagaad Base, Lower Shabelle (late March 2026); strategic al-Shabaab logistics hub for attacks on Mogadishu

label: Golis/Cal Miskaad Mtns (ISIS-Somalia)
sub: ISIS-Somalia base area in Puntland's Bari Region; AFRICOM conducted multiple airstrikes here in March 2026 targeting ISIS-Somalia cave complexes; authorized by SecDef Hegseth

label: Mubarak (SNA/AUSSOM Battle, Mar 26)
sub: Site of SNA and Ugandan AUSSOM repulsion of Al-Shabaab assault on March 26, 2026; 40 militants killed. Mubarak was recently recaptured in Operation Rolling Thunder/Hilaac and serves as a staging base Al-Shabaab used for attacks on Mogadishu via the southwest corridor.

label: Daynuunay (SNA Kills 21 AS, Mar 29)
sub: Al-Shabaab assaulted SNA positions at Daynuunay on the Baidoa–Mogadishu road corridor (Bay Region) on March 29, 2026; SNA killed 21 militants and wounded 10+. Strategically critical node for federal logistics to Southwest State.

label: Baidoa (FGS Seizure, Mar 30, 2026)
sub: SNA forces and 'SW Salvation' allies seized Baidoa (SW State capital) on March 30, 2026 following constitutional standoff. SW State President Laftagareen resigned. UNHCR confirmed 50,000+ civilians newly displaced. First FGS military takeover of a federal member state capital.

label: Kunya Barrow (NISA Op, Apr 4, 2026)
sub: NISA and international partners killed 6 Al-Shabaab fighters and destroyed an IED manufacturing facility (engines for VBIED/mine production) at Kunya Barrow, Lower Shabelle, on April 4, 2026. A second phase killed 3 more militants in Togarey forests. Total: 9 killed.

label: Mubarak (AUSSOM/SNA Commander Capture, Apr 3)
sub: Joint UPDF AUSSOM and SNA Gorgor Commando (203rd Unit, 20th Brigade) operation captured senior Al-Shabaab battalion commander Salaad Cusmaan Macalin ('Sahm') at northwest Mubarak on April 3, 2026. Commander was scouting attack positions; 17 foreign fighters (from Jilib) were in his group. Suicide bomber associate also revealed.

label: Abdow Dibille Camp (Col. Nur Farey Killed, Apr 5)
sub: SNA 14th October Brigade commander Col. Nur Farey assassinated on April 5, 2026 at Abdow Dibille military camp near Afgooye, Lower Shabelle, by a gunman in military uniform suspected to be an Al-Shabaab infiltrator. Farey was addressing troops when killed. Investigation underway.

label: Juba Regions Offensive (Apr 15–18, 2026)
sub: SNA and Jubbaland forces conducted a large-scale joint offensive in the Juba regions April 15–18, 2026. Airstrikes with 'international support' killed 54 Al-Shabaab (Apr 15-16); ground operations killed 27 more (Apr 18). Operations centered near Jilib/Middle Jubba Al-Shabaab stronghold. Most significant Jubbaland theater action since late 2023.

label: NISA Lower Shabelle Op (Apr 16, 2026)
sub: NISA targeted operation killed 9 Al-Shabaab militants in Lower Shabelle on April 16, 2026. Intelligence-led raid separate from Jubbaland airstrike campaign; part of ongoing HVT strategy in the southwest corridor.

label: Adan Yabal (AS Seizure, Apr 19, 2026)
sub: Al-Shabaab seized Adan Yabal (Aadan Yabaal) in Hiiraan Region on April 19, 2026, in heavy clashes with the SNA. Al-Shabaab also killed 8 civilians including 5 women in the area. President Mohamud ordered an emergency SNA offensive to retake the town. Major setback in a region where the 2022 Macawisley-SNA offensive had made significant gains.

label: ISIS-Somalia Strike (AFRICOM #56, Apr 17, 2026)
sub: AFRICOM conducted its 56th Somalia airstrike of 2026 on April 17 (announced April 19) targeting ISIS-Somalia approximately 30 miles (48 km) southeast of Bosaso, Puntland. Part of the intensified campaign against ISIS-Somalia cave complexes in the Golis/Cal Miskaad mountains. 2026 pace tracking to 200+ annual strikes.

label: Guulane (NISA Raid, Apr 22, 2026)
sub: NISA and international partners killed 33 Al-Shabaab fighters in a two-phase precision operation at Guulane village, Middle Shabelle, on April 22, 2026. First strike killed 15 including senior commanders; follow-up killed 18 more. ~90 militants had assembled to plan new attacks.

label: Wadajir Area (AFRICOM Strike #57, Apr 21)
sub: Target of AFRICOM's 57th airstrike in Somalia in 2026 (AFRICOM PR #36382). Al-Shabaab fighters approximately 90 km southwest of Kismayo, Jubbaland. Strike conducted in coordination with the Federal Government of Somalia.

label: Xaafuun Piracy Incident (Apr 22, 2026)
sub: EU NAVFOR Atalanta investigating possible armed boarding of a product tanker approximately 24 nautical miles southeast of Xaafuun (Hafun), the tip of the Horn of Africa. Reported by Diaplous Group security consultants; part of resurgent Somali piracy pattern active since late October 2025.
